trade.summary: Bilateral trade is modest but growing. Israel exports chemicals, electronic equipment, and defense-related goods; Mexico exports auto parts, agricultural products, and manufactured goods. Geographic distance and limited trade infrastructure constrain deeper commerce.

diplomatic.summary: Mexico and Israel maintain diplomatic relations but ties are not especially close. Mexico has historically taken pro-Palestinian positions in the UN and has been critical of Israeli military actions in Gaza. Relations were further strained in 2024 when Mexico's government condemned Israeli operations more vocally than most Latin American peers.

military.summary: Limited military ties. Mexico has historically purchased some Israeli surveillance and security technology, though this became politically sensitive. No formal alliance or exercises.

financial.summary: Limited bilateral investment. Some Israeli tech companies have operations in Mexico for the Latin American market; Mexican firms have minimal Israeli presence. Financial ties remain thin.

energy.summary: Negligible direct bilateral energy trade. No significant energy partnership.

scientific.summary: Moderate scientific cooperation, primarily in agriculture and water management where Israeli expertise benefits Mexican needs. Some bilateral academic exchange programs exist.
